title: OSCP+ / OSCP
description: 🎯 OSCP / OSCP+ – Offensive Security Certified Professional (Plus)  An advanced, hands-on certification built on the OSCP foundation with extra emphasis on realistic, multi-step attack paths.
markdown: 🛡️ **OSCP / OSCP+ – Offensive Security Certified Professional (Plus)** The **OSCP+** represents one of the most **respected and demanding certifications** in offensive security.\ It’s designed to validate a professional’s ability to perform **real-world penetration tests** from start to finish — from initial reconnaissance to privilege escalation, lateral movement, and full documentation. Unlike theoretical exams, the OSCP+ is entirely **hands-on**, simulating a realistic enterprise environment filled with modern security layers and diverse attack paths. It tests not only technical expertise but also methodology, endurance, and problem-solving under time pressure. *** 🧪 **Exam Format & Flow**\ The exam spans a total of **48 hours**: * **24 hours for exploitation**, where you must identify, exploit, and escalate privileges across multiple machines — including both standalone and Active Directory targets. * **24 hours for reporting**, where you prepare a professional, client-style penetration test report detailing every step, finding, and mitigation. Each target is unique — combining **network, web, and OS-level vulnerabilities** that must be chained together. Success requires deep enumeration, creative exploitation, and strategic pivoting through internal systems. *** 🧠 **Key Skills Practiced** * **In-depth Enumeration:** Extracting critical data from ports, services, and misconfigurations using Nmap, SMB tools, and manual analysis. * **Exploitation Mastery:** Buffer overflow attacks, privilege escalation, and abusing insecure configurations in both Windows and Linux. * **Active Directory Attacks:** Kerberoasting, AS-REP roasting, constrained delegation abuse, and other AD-specific privilege escalation techniques. * **Pivoting & Post-Exploitation:** Establishing tunnels, routing traffic, and compromising internal networks beyond the initial foothold. * **Professional Reporting:** Translating technical findings into a structured, business-focused report that mirrors real consultancy deliverables. *** 🧰 **Tools & Methodology** * **Nmap, Enum4Linux, CrackMapExec** – for discovery and initial footholds. * **Burp Suite, Responder, Impacket, and PowerShell scripts** – for exploitation and lateral movement. * **BloodHound** – for AD graph analysis and privilege escalation paths. * **Manual analysis** – no overreliance on tools; every action justified by logic and observation. *** 🧩 **Takeaway**\ The **OSCP+ journey** reshaped how I think as a penetration tester.\ It taught me to stay patient when enumeration feels endless, creative when exploits fail, and disciplined when documenting complex attack paths. More than a technical exam, it’s a **test of mindset and persistence** — one that builds confidence to handle real-world red team operations and enterprise-scale assessments. Completing OSCP+ means more than earning a certification; it’s proof of **methodology, endurance, and true offensive capability** under pressure.\ It stands as a **milestone achievement** in the career of anyone pursuing excellence in ethical hacking and penetration testing. *** ### 📚 Study & Preparation Roadmap Here is my recommended study flow for candidates preparing for the **OSCP / OSCP+**: 1. **Foundational Prep**: - Work through the official **OffSec PEN-200** course labs. Aim to compromise at least 40-50 standalone machines and complete all AD sets. - Use **TryHackMe**'s *Jr. Penetration Tester* and *Offensive Pentesting* paths to build basic exploit and enumeration habits. 2. **Practice Platforms**: - **Proving Grounds (PG) Practice**: OffSec's own machines are the closest to the exam style. Focus on intermediate boxes (e.g. *TJ_Null's OSCP List*). - **Hack The Box (HTB)**: Work on OSCP-like machines using community lists. 3. **Active Directory Focus**: - Master Kerberoasting, AS-REP Roasting, Pass-the-Hash, Pass-the-Ticket, and GPO/ACL abuse. - Practice using `BloodHound` to map paths and `Impacket` tools (`secretsdump`, `psexec`, `wmiexec`) for execution. 4. **Pivoting & Networking**: - Master pivoting tools like `Chisel` and `Ligolo-ng`. Ensure you can pivot through double-nested networks effortlessly. 5. **Exam Day Tips**: - **Document continuously**: Take screenshots of every flag, exploit command, and credential. - **Time management**: If stuck on a single vector for >2 hours, pivot to another machine or take a 15-minute walk. Fresh eyes solve hard boxes.
